q: What is the star at the centre of our solar system?
exp: The Sun is a star and holds 99.8% of the solar system's mass.

q: Which gas do we breathe from the air to live?
exp: Air is about 21% oxygen; most of the rest is nitrogen.

q: What state is water in when it freezes?
exp: Water freezes at 0 °C, becoming solid ice.

q: What makes objects fall to the ground?
exp: Gravity pulls objects toward the centre of the Earth.

q: How many legs does an insect have?
exp: All insects have 6 legs; spiders (with 8) are not insects.

q: Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
exp: Mars looks red because of iron oxide (rust) in its soil.

q: Which part of a plant absorbs water from the soil?
exp: Roots absorb water and nutrients and anchor the plant.

q: Water is made of oxygen and which other element?
exp: Water's formula is H₂O: two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen.
The tongue has thousands of taste buds that detect flavours.

q: Which object lights the night and orbits the Earth?
exp: The Moon has no light of its own — it reflects the Sun's light.

q: What do plants use to make their own food?
exp: In photosynthesis, plants use sunlight, water and CO₂ to make food.

q: Which of these is a mammal?
exp: Dolphins breathe air and feed their young milk — they are mammals, not fish.
